summary: freertc postinstall rewrites installer's package.json to pin freertc to "latest" and re-runs npm install
details: On install, scripts/postinstall-message.mjs locates the consuming project's package.json via process.env.INIT_CWD and, if it finds `freertc` in dependencies/devDependencies/optionalDependencies, overwrites the version specifier to the literal string "latest" (e.g., `table.freertc = 'latest'`), writes the manifest back to disk, and then spawns `npm install` in the host project. This silently strips the installer's semver pinning and forces every subsequent install in that project to fetch whatever version the author most recently published. The installer never consented to convert their pinned dependency into a live auto-update channel; lockfile/pinning protections are destroyed without disclosure. This grants the author a perpetual, unilateral code-execution channel into every downstream project that has ever installed this package: any future release (malicious or otherwise) will be pulled automatically on the next install, and the recursive `npm install` inside postinstall accelerates propagation. This is a supply-chain attack mechanism — it produces a durable attacker benefit (guaranteed delivery of future arbitrary code to all prior installers) at install time, regardless of the contents of this specific version.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
